claim: Valid FASTQ structure and paired-read synchronization must be established before mutating preprocessing stages.
Adapter evidence is an observed QC signal and is not equivalent to trimming unless a downstream trimming policy consumes the adapter bank.
General read trimming may change sequence length, base composition, and read retention; comparability requires fixed tool, adapter bank, quality policy, and minimum-length policy.
Filtering removes or retains reads under explicit N-content, quality, complexity, and length policies and must publish read-count accounting.
Error correction can alter bases and is not safe as a default for every library type; use requires route-family justification and tool-specific evidence.
UMI extraction must occur before any stage that can trim, rename, or otherwise destroy barcode-position evidence unless the route explicitly proves post-trim recovery.
Merged-read outputs are not interchangeable with paired-end outputs unless downstream stage contracts explicitly admit merged-read layout.
FASTQ duplicate removal is not equivalent to alignment-aware duplicate marking and must not be interpreted as a BAM duplicate metric.
Low-complexity filtering changes read retention using explicit entropy, k-mer, or motif policy and requires fixed thresholds for fair comparison.
Host depletion is database-dependent; source reference, build, index backend, and index digest define the scientific comparability boundary.
Reference contaminant depletion requires locked contaminant bundle identity, index digest, and backend policy before outputs can be compared.
rRNA depletion is comparable only when source sequence collection, index digest, and SortMeRNA policy are locked.
Taxonomic screening is a database-bound classifier report; scientific interpretation requires locked database sources, taxonomy dump, build command, and classifier policy.
Read profiling is observational and must not mutate FASTQ payloads.
Read-length profiles are descriptive summaries and must be linked to exact input hashes and histogram policy.
Overrepresented-sequence profiling reports sequence enrichment signals and must not be treated as adapter or contaminant removal.
PolyG/PolyX tail trimming is platform- and motif-policy dependent and must not run by default for libraries without supporting instrument evidence.
Terminal-damage trimming is route-family-specific and must be restricted to ancient-DNA or explicit damage-aware plans.
Primer normalization is amplicon-assay specific and requires locked primer bank, orientation policy, and mismatch policy.
Chimera removal is benchmark-comparable only within the same amplicon assay, denoising context, reference policy, and chimera-detection backend.
Reference indexing is comparable only when source reference, index backend, backend version, and emitted index digest are fixed.
ASV inference is comparable only within fixed amplicon assay, denoising method, pooling policy, and chimera policy.
OTU clustering is not equivalent to ASV inference and requires fixed identity threshold, representative policy, and clustering backend.
Abundance normalization changes table scale and must publish method identity before downstream comparison.
QC reporting aggregates governed reports and must preserve source report checksums rather than becoming the sole evidence surface.
Taxonomy database builds require locked source libraries, taxonomy dump, build command, and emitted database digest.
rRNA database builds require locked source sequence collection, build command, and emitted index digest.
Contaminant database builds require locked contaminant source bundle, build command, backend version, and emitted index digest.
Host reference bundles require source reference identity, alias policy, index compatibility, and bundle digest before host-depletion use.
Adapter-bank preparation requires locked sequence records, orientation policy, source authority, and record digest.
Primer-bank preparation requires assay family, primer records, orientation policy, mismatch policy, and record digest.
Pair repair changes read ordering and must publish orphan accounting before pair-sensitive stages.
Lane concatenation must occur before sample-level preprocessing and requires stable lane ordering and sample identity preservation.
Demultiplexing requires locked barcode manifests and mismatch policy before sample-level FASTQ claims can be made.
Subsampling is benchmark-comparable only when seed, fraction or target count, and input checksums are fixed.
Interleaving is layout-only and must preserve pair identity and pair order.
Deinterleaving is layout-only and must preserve pair identity and pair order.
Read-name normalization is unsafe for provenance-sensitive workflows unless original headers remain recoverable.
Layout classification must distinguish paired, single-end, interleaved, and premerged inputs before route safety rules are applied.
Instrument-artifact detection is report-only unless a governed route consumes the artifact class.
Prealignment complexity estimates are exploratory unless benchmarked against aligned duplicate metrics.
Premerge duplicate signals are not equivalent to aligned duplicate marking and must remain report-only until route evidence exists.
QC manifest materialization binds report checksums, input hashes, tool identities, and asset identities before aggregation.
Asset verification must fail closed for world-class-closed plans when any declared asset lock is missing or mismatched.
Plan certification requires a provenance snapshot binding stage graph, inputs, tools, containers, runtime assets, and checksums.
